slides.title: Conditional Query Parameters for CoAP Observe
CoAP Extensions for Asynchronous Task Resources

chat.author: Christian Amsüss
chat.text: My comment on KUDOS will  be editorial enough to just take it to the list.l

chat.author: Christian Amsüss
chat.text: ("please rename the x byte to something related to what it does")

chat.author: Alexander Pelov
chat.text:
I would be interested in giving a try on implementing this. 
@Marco, is there a git repo I can clone and try to run some code against?

chat.author: Marco Tiloca
chat.text: @Alex, that would be great! Please see the repo that was linked in slide 10: https://github.com/adriankastrati/californium-thesis-work/tree/multicast-notifications

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: We have Java now, so C, C++ or Rust are still available ;-)

chat.author: Alexander Pelov
chat.text: does Python make sense?

chat.author: Marco Tiloca
chat.text: Sure

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: Also: are we happy to keep the last option on the backburner - yes ok for me! We can describe its general design.

chat.author: Christian Amsüss
chat.text: Correcting myself from earlier: The option for unicast sensibility is in multicast-proxy; the options in Onion OSCORE are related but not right the ones we need here.

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: @christian - is there a 'sensibility' option? (Or was 'leisure' intended?)

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: meetecho: speaker?

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Thanks!

chat.author: Jaime Jimenez
chat.text: silly question (no mic needed, just chat). Is SID+DNS intended for local/private networks (DNS-SD, mDNS) or Internet-wide too? I would assume DNS folks may complain of this use.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: We'll have to wrap up with this subject

chat.author: Maria Matějka
chat.text:
thinking through the SID-DNS over and over again, i'm still convinced that as soon as a device is sold to a customer, it should be self-contained enough to allow querying its own yang module and sid file directly
i know that it needs some bytes but consider that both files can be pre-compressed, and generally the scale of the code is at best linear with the size of the YANG model and SID file
all in all, i don't expect the inclusion of the sidfile and yang module to eat a significant amount of rom/nvram/flash compared to the necessary parser/encoder code

chat.author: Laurent Toutain
chat.text: Yes the device, but the one that receives the message from the device

chat.author: Laurent Toutain
chat.text: does it have to be configured with all the SID files to understand the message

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: I just mailed the list - seeing some similarity between the 'task' lifecycle and PubSub https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-core-coap-pubsub-21#name-topic-lifecycle

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: (even though different)

chat.author: Christian Amsüss
chat.text: It's different but there is the similarity that beyond the CRUD pattern, it has a pair resources that, in Linzhe's case, might be "instructions" and "state" and "outcome"

chat.author: Jaime Jimenez
chat.text: We also had that pattern (create a resource for async check the state of an operation) in lwm2m long ago if i remember right. it is a common pattern

chat.author: Maria Matějka
chat.text:
Laurent Toutain said:

does it have to be configured with all the SID files to understand the message

if it receives a message from a device, it should be able to query the same device for the sid file and yang module

chat.author: Jaime Jimenez
chat.text: coap has the beauty of observe + query param to also be notified instead of polling. very neat

chat.author: Martine Lenders
chat.text: Will definitely have a look at CoAP Message Diagnostic Notation

chat.author: Jaime Jimenez
chat.text: nobody knows btw the DNS-SD vs DNS

chat.author: Jaime Jimenez
chat.text: and SID?

chat.author: Laurent Toutain
chat.text: @maria a SID file can be very big and in a constrained environment it may be difficult to get.

chat.author: Laurent Toutain
chat.text: bye
